spec_match.rationale: Although the layout uses calc() with --p dynamically, lines 20 and 21 contain highly broken, unparseable CSS syntax (multiplying color variables with percentages outside of color-mix/calc, with mismatched parentheses).

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive. Users can scroll inside a viewport to trigger scroll-driven progress animation, or use a simulated fallback range slider manually.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Excellent explanation of compositor-friendly scroll interpolation benefits.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes a CSS.supports() capability badge with dual polyfill/fallback support via JS.

references.rationale: Includes valid spec references and ChromeStatus links.

design_system.rationale: Mostly consistent with layout tokens, though the broken CSS color variables are discarded.

openQuestions.title: Fix broken header background-color and color styles
openQuestions.detail: Lines 20 and 21 contain mismatched parentheses and invalid CSS arithmetic: `background-color: var(--accent-blue) * 35%));` and `color: var(--accent-blue)) * 80%));`. These lines fail to parse, resulting in missing color transitions. Please fix them to use valid CSS color-mix or calc forms (like the clean `hsl` calculation shown in the code card on line 85).

summary: A beautifully structured scroll headroom showcase that supports both scrolling viewport triggers and range selectors. However, it suffers from severe syntax bugs on lines 20 and 21 that render the dynamic color transitions completely broken.
